description: Figure 5 presents the microstructural characterization of the intercritically annealed Fe-8Mn-3Al-0.2C steel (M1-IA). Panel (a) comprises four secondary-electron SEM micrographs (a1–a4) at progressively higher magnifications with scale bars of 20 μm, 5 μm, 3 μm, and 2 μm, respectively, revealing a dual-phase microstructure consisting of polygonal α-ferrite (marked α, appearing as relatively smooth regions) and a fine lamellar α′+γ constituent (marked α′+γ). The images indicate negligible martensite, consistent with intercritical annealing at 700 °C for 1 h followed by water quenching. Panel (b) is an EBSD phase map of the same M1-IA sample, where the orange-colored phase represents austenite distributed within the ferrite matrix. Together, these images confirm that the M1-IA sample contains only α-ferrite and austenite without δ-ferrite. Citation text reports quantitative phase analysis yields 45 % retained austenite in M1-IA, substantially higher than the 19 % reported for the Si-bearing M2-IA counterpart, supporting the conclusion that the absence of Si prevents equilibrium δ-ferrite formation and maximizes austenite fraction and stability.
